reasoning: The resolution is based on the stock price relative to the October 24, 2025 close of $68.19. Weekly short-term stock movements are nearly random walks, centering our base rate around 0.50. There are two immediate catalysts: (1) The Q3 earnings report on October 28, which introduces high variance but has a neutral expected value (slight negative EPS surprise offset by general growth expectations and a history of earnings beats). (2) Most importantly, the company declared an increased $0.36 quarterly dividend with a record date of Friday, October 31. This makes October 31 the exact ex-dividend date (under T+1 settlement). Since November 2 is a Sunday, its resolution will use the October 31 close price, which will be artificially depressed by the $0.36 dividend distribution (a ~0.5% drag). This known negative price pressure on the exact resolution day slightly pulls the probability below 50% for the first date. For the later resolution dates (November 25 and January 24), the ex-dividend drop becomes negligible compared to accumulated variance and background market drift, so the probability mean-reverts back to 0.50, reflecting increasing uncertainty over longer time horizons.
